title: Two distinct users cannot use podman on the same Windows PC with Hyper-V
body:
### Issue Description

Starting an Hyper-V machine, on a Windows PC where another user has already created one, fails with error:

> `Error: Illegal argument (key already exists?) (32773)`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Set `$CONTAINER_MACHINE_PROVIDER=hyper` and run `podman machine init --now` as a first user
2. Login as a second user and run the same commands

### Describe the results you received

When the second user starts the machine, it fails:

```log

```

### Describe the results you expected

Running a second machine as a distinct user should work.

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
I'd be very interested if this works, too. It can't work with WSL, because it's a per-user application, but Hyper-V should be able to get shared across multiple users.

How does that work? :D
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@l0rd i guess this can be closed. I went to this as it had the same error code we're facing with ignition failure but it's not possible to create a machine having the same name on two distinct users anymore bc of https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/cmd/podman/machine/init.go#L223
